app.title: Image UltraHDR Mask Drawing Tool
shortcuts.tools: Shortcuts: B for brush, E for eraser
auto.title: Auto Draw
edit.title: Edit
preview.title: UltraHDR Preview
preview.description: This preview is generated from the current mask so you can inspect the export result before continuing to adjust it.
preview.emptyInitial: Upload an image to show the current UltraHDR preview here.
preview.emptyBeforeMask: Upload an image and draw a mask to show the UltraHDR preview here.
preview.emptyUploaded: Image uploaded. The current UltraHDR preview will appear after you start drawing the mask.
preview.liveOffManual: Live preview is disabled. Click "Refresh Preview" to generate it manually.
preview.liveOffAfterUpload: Live preview is disabled. Click "Refresh Preview" to generate it manually.
preview.updated: The UltraHDR preview for the current mask has been updated.
preview.failedStatus: Preview generation failed. Keep editing and try again.
hdrTest.title: UltraHDR Display Test
hdrTest.description: If you cannot see any pattern in the image below, your browser or device likely does not support UltraHDR display. Try viewing it on an HDR-capable device or display with system HDR enabled.
instructions.title: Instructions
instructions.step1: Click "Choose Image" to upload an image
instructions.step2: Draw a black-and-white mask on the image with the mouse. Black areas are opaque, white areas are transparent.
instructions.step3: Use brush, eraser, undo, and redo to fine-tune the mask
instructions.step4: Adjust brush size and opacity to get the desired result
instructions.step5: Use "Clear Mask" to clear the current mask
instructions.step6: Use "Reset Image" to upload another image
instructions.step7: Click "Export UltraHDR" to merge the mask as a gain map with the source image and download it
thresholdPreview.empty: Upload an image to show the threshold binary preview here.
status.imageUploaded: Image uploaded. You can now draw the mask.
status.autoBrightPercent: Bright area mask drawn with luminance-rank percent mapping
status.needImageAndMask: Upload an image and create a mask first
